mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically boosting forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in unemployment signals deteriorating labor demand, triggering recession fears that compress equity valuations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces housing starts, it indicates a supply-demand imbalance favoring corporate earnings expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO minus HOUST) shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, persistent price pressures force the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL remains above the threshold for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and consumer credit balances reflect robust household spending, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial output below its long-term baseline signals weakening manufacturing demand, which historically precedes equity drawdowns and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO breaches the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: When strong non-farm payroll growth coincides with elevated federal funds rates, wage-push inflation pressures dominate, forcing prolonged restrictive policy that suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and high FEDFUNDS does not predict negative 20-day returns (t-stat < -1.5).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery phase, rapid job creation validates economic resilience, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive when job growth exceeds the threshold during recovery phases.
